lede: A look at April 17 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Antonio Nusa and Chuck Kobasew, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Death and funeral of Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh
events.desc: The funeral of Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh, takes place at St George's Chapel, Windsor Castle.

events.title: NASA
events.desc: NASA's Kepler space telescope confirms the discovery of the first Earth-size planet in the habitable zone of another star.

events.title: West Fertilizer Company explosion
events.desc: An explosion at a fertilizer plant in the city of West, Texas, kills 15 people and injures 160 others.

events.title: Provisional Government of Bangladesh
events.desc: The Provisional Government of Bangladesh is formed.

events.title: Sirhan Sirhan
events.desc: Sirhan Sirhan is convicted of assassinating Robert F. Kennedy.

events.title: Communist Party of Czechoslovakia
events.desc: Communist Party of Czechoslovakia chairman Alexander Dubček is deposed.

events.title: Peak District
events.desc: The Peak District becomes the United Kingdom's first National Park.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Axis powers invasion of Yugoslavia is completed when it signs an armistice with Germany and Italy.

events.title: Communist Party of Korea
events.desc: The Communist Party of Korea (CPK) was founded in Japanese-ruled Korea (Chōsen) in Keijō (now Seoul) by Kim Yong-bom and Pak Hon-yong.

events.title: Virginia
events.desc: The state of Virginia's secession convention votes to secede from the United States; Virginia later becomes the eighth state to join the Confederate States of America.

deaths.text: Yngve Moe, Norwegian bass player and songwriter
Marcel Dassault, French businessman, founded Dassault Aviation
Aleksandr Golovin (artist), Russian painter and stage designer

observances.text: Evacuation Day (Syria), celebrates the recognition of the independence of Syria from France in 1946.
